title: PRISE Lab
text:
Policy Research in Sustainability and Economics
UT Dallas · School of Economic, Political and Policy Sciences

title: PRISE Lab
text:
About
PRISE is a research hub at UT Dallas dedicated to understanding and solving critical challenges in environmental sustainability, energy, and economic inequality. We focus on the human impact of environmental and economic shocks, particularly how they affect marginalized populations.
Our research spans hurricane impacts in the U.S., tropical forest losses, energy access in sub-Saharan Africa, and pollution effects worldwide.

title: PRISE Lab
text:
Research Areas

Climate and Political Accountability
How extreme weather events shape election outcomes and climate policy.


Conservation Program Effectiveness
Optimizing conservation funding — evidence from Vietnam and beyond.


Clean Energy Adoption
Household adoption of energy-efficient cooking appliances in Uganda.


Land-Use Change and Deforestation
Political economy of deforestation in Brazil and Indonesia using remote sensing and causal analysis.

title: Team
text:
Applied economist
Environmental, development, and energy economics. Research across 11 countries in Africa and Latin America. Affiliated with Duke Energy Access Project and Environment for Development.
Website






Political economist
Political economy of land-use change and deforestation in Brazil and Indonesia. Remote sensing and causal analysis methods.

title: Team
text:
Student Researchers
We are always looking for motivated students. If you are interested in joining the lab, reach out to either director.

title: Research
text:
Extreme weather events → election outcomes and climate policy. How do environmental shocks affect political behavior and policy preferences?



Optimizing funding for conservation programs. Evidence from Vietnam using quasi-experimental methods.



Household adoption of energy-efficient cooking appliances in Uganda. Understanding barriers and incentives for clean energy transitions.



Political economy of deforestation in Brazil and Indonesia. Combining remote sensing data with causal inference methods to evaluate conservation policy.



Testing water quality across UT Dallas campus for PFAS, lead, and other contaminants. Estimating willingness to pay for improvements using a discrete choice experiment. Funded by the UTD Green Fund.
